FAQ'S
PAST WINNERS

ENTER NOW

THE WORLD INTERIOR NEWS AWARDS

CELEBRATING DESIGN EXCELLENCE

The World Interiors News Awards is a prestigious global competition celebrating the best in interior design.

This annual event unites the interior design community, offering a unique platform for industry recognition, innovation and the opportunity to influence the future of design worldwide. 

Have you had a groundbreaking project across residential, commercial, or public spaces? Then make sure you enter the WIN Awards 2026.







START YOUR ENTRY HERE
DOWNLOAD THE ENTRY KIT
KEY INFORMATION

First Entry Deadline: Thursday 7 May | £410 +VAT

Second Entry Deadline: Thursday 28 May | £520 +VAT



Shortlist Announcement: October 2026

Winners Announcement: November 2026

WHY ENTER?
OUR 2026 CATEGORIES
27 DIVERSE CATEGORIES COVERING ALL ASPECTS OF INTERIOR DESIGN
Public & Cultural Interiors
Entertainment & Performance Spaces

Recognises excellence in the interior design of theatres, concert halls, museums, galleries, cinemas, and immersive spaces that inspire and engage audiences.

Rewards:
- Creative spatial storytelling
- Acoustic and technical innovation
- Visitor experience and accessibility
- Cultural and community impact

Education

Celebrates outstanding interiors that support learning in schools, universities, libraries, and training facilities.

Rewards:
- Flexible, future-proof design
- Support for wellbeing and engagement
- Innovative use of technology
- Inclusivity and accessibility

Transport

Honours exceptional interior design in airports, stations, terminals, and related infrastructure enhancing passenger experience and interior design.

Rewards:
- Efficient wayfinding and flow
- Durability and safety
- Integration with local culture
- Sustainable materials and operation

Civic

Awards interior design excellence in government buildings, courts, community centres, and public institutions.

Rewards:
- Transparency and openness
- Accessibility and inclusivity
- Durable, functional design
- Civic identity and pride

Historic

Celebrates sensitive preservation and restoration of interiors with heritage significance.

Rewards:
- Respect for original fabric
- Adaptive integration of new elements
- Craftsmanship and materiality
- Cultural and historical narrative

Hospitality Interiors
Restaurants & Cafes

Recognises inspiring hospitality interiors that create memorable dining experiences.

Rewards:
- Atmosphere and branding synergy
- Functional flow and comfort
- Innovative material use
- Sustainability and operational efficiency

Hotel & Other Overnight Accommodation

Awards excellence in guest accommodation interiors including hotels, resorts, and boutique lodgings.

Rewards:
- Guest comfort and wellbeing
- Distinctive identity and storytelling
- Sustainability and durability
- Innovative amenities and technology

Members Clubs

Honours interior design excellence in private members’ clubs and exclusive social venues.

Rewards:
- Sophisticated atmosphere and privacy
- Functional layout for social interaction
- Integration of luxury and comfort
- Attention to detail and craftsmanship

Sports & Wellness Interiors
Sports, Spas & Wellness Spaces

Recognises interiors supporting athletic, spa, and wellness activities that promote health and relaxation.

Rewards:
- Functional and flexible programming
- Atmosphere of calm and energy
- Use of natural materials and light
- Wellbeing focus

Hospitals & Clinics

Awards interior environments in healthcare that enhance patient care and staff wellbeing.

Rewards:
- Infection control and safety
- Comfort and calming design
- Functional efficiency
- Accessibility and inclusive design

Workspace Interiors
Corporate Offices (Under 5,000 sqm)

Recognises innovative workplace interiors for smaller office environments.

Rewards:
- Space efficiency and flexibility
- Employee wellbeing and collaboration
- Brand integration
- Thoughtful zoning and flow
- Integration of technology and amenities


Corporate Offices (5,000 - 10,000 sqm)

Celebrates medium-sized office interiors that balance scale with user experience.

Rewards:
- Space efficiency and flexibility
- Employee wellbeing and collaboration
- Brand integration
- Thoughtful zoning and flow
- Integration of technology and amenities

Corporate Offices (Over 10,000 sqm)

Honours large-scale workplace interiors demonstrating leadership in design innovation and sustainability.

Rewards:
- Space efficiency and flexibility
- Employee wellbeing and collaboration
- Brand integration
- Thoughtful zoning and flow
- Integration of technology and amenities

Studios, Co-Working Spaces & Home Offices

Recognises creative and flexible internal working environments.

Rewards:
- Adaptability and user comfort
- Creative identity and branding
- Efficient use of space
- Support for wellbeing and productivity

Communal Areas

Celebrates internal shared spaces within residential, workplace, or mixed-use buildings fostering community and interaction.

- Inviting and functional design
- Integration of technology and amenities
- Encouragement of social connection

Residential Interiors
One-off Homes (Under £1M)

Recognizes individually designed private residences under £1 million that demonstrate exceptional interior design, celebrating creativity, spatial planning, materiality, and functional living solutions tailored to the client’s lifestyle.

Rewards:
- Thoughtful spatial planning
- Material quality and detail
- Personalized lifestyle expression / support
- Innovative use of space

One-off Homes (Over £1M)

Recognises bespoke private residences over £1 million that exemplify outstanding interior design, highlighting refined material palettes, spatial flow, craftsmanship, and thoughtfully curated environments that elevate everyday living.

Rewards:
- Thoughtful spatial planning
- Material quality and detail
- Personalized lifestyle expression / support
- Innovative use of space

Show Flats & Developments

Honours model homes and apartment interiors that inspire buyers through style and functionality.

Rewards:
- Smart space optimisation
- Visual appeal and branding


Residential Developments (Apartments)

Recognises large-scale apartment developments that prioritise exceptional interior design, seamlessly combining aesthetic coherence, intelligent space planning, high-quality finishes, and resident-focused functionality to enhance comfort, wellbeing, and liveability.

Rewards:
- Cohesive design language
- Functional common areas
- Community-focused amenities

Specialist Interiors
Lighting Projects
Awards innovative use of interior lighting design that enhances space, mood, and function.

Rewards:
- Creativity in lighting concepts
- Technical excellence and efficiency
- Integration with architecture and interiors




Temporary, Experiential Projects & Pop Ups

Celebrates short-term or immersive interiors that create unique, memorable experiences.

Rewards:
- Innovation and originality
- Impactful storytelling
- Flexibility and build efficiency
- Engagement and visitor interaction

CGI & Visualisation

Recognises excellence in digital renderings, animations, and visual storytelling for interior projects.

Rewards:
- Creativity and composition
- Effective communication of design intent
- Technical sophistication

Excellence in Sustainable Design

Awards projects and practices that embed sustainability deeply into interior design with measurable impact.

- Carbon reduction and energy efficiency
- Responsible sourcing and circularity
- Social responsibility
- Transparent reporting and innovation
- ESG Integration
- Measurable sustainable outcomes

Excellence in Inclusive Design

Honours designs that prioritise inclusivity and accessibility across all types of interiors.

Rewards:
- User-centric approach
- Compliance with accessibility standards
- Enhancing user independence

Reuse & Refurbishment Projects

Honours exceptional transformation of existing interiors through retrofit, conservation, and creative reinvention.

Rewards:
- Respect for original structure and fabric
- Innovative spatial adaptation
- Design-led sustainability
- Cultural and functional revitalisation

Retail & Consumer Design Interiors
Independent Shops & Small Chains

Recognises retail interiors for small-scale or independent businesses that deliver distinctive customer experiences.

Rewards:
- Brand identity and storytelling
- Functional layout and flow
- Materiality and craftsmanship
- Community and customer engagement

Retails Chains, Department Stores & Shopping Centres

Celebrates large-scale retail environments distinguished by exceptional interior design, where innovative spatial concepts, immersive customer experiences, materiality, lighting, and brand-led storytelling combine to drive both commercial performance and memorable engagement.

Rewards:
- Consistent brand experience
- Efficient circulation and wayfinding
- Visual merchandising integration

Grand Prix
Judges Awards / ‘Grand Prix’ - The winners of any of the above pay-to-enter categories will be automatically entered into the below categories.
Emerging Interiors Practice of the Year
Interiors Practice of the Year
Outstanding Interiors Project of the Year
NOMINATE A JUDGE

We invite you to nominate yourself or a colleague using the form below.

The final judging panel will be comprised of an inclusive range of individuals to fully represent the industry. 



Nominate a Judge Here
HEAR FROM OUR EDITOR

HELEN PARTON

World Interior News Editor

"The 2026 World Interior News Awards are the perfect platform for architects and designers to shout about their most compelling work, across a huge range of typologies and project scales. Whether you're behind a boutique hospitality concept or a large-scale commercial transformation, the WIN Awards are here to make sure outstanding design gets the recognition it deserves. It's your chance to get your project in front of one of the industry's most respected judging panels, gaining visibility among peers, collaborators, and potential clients across the world!"

CONTACT US
Morgan Lovegrove
Event Manager

E: morgan.lovegrove@haymarket.com

P: +44 (0) 7508787310

Michael Baker
Sponsorship Opportunities

E: michael.baker@haymarket.com

KEEP UP-TO-DATE WITH WORLD INTERIORS NEWS ON SOCIALS

规则.text:
ENTRY ELIGIBILITY

Open to: Interior designers, architects, developers, retailers, hoteliers, restaurateurs, manufacturers, and suppliers.



Project Eligibility: Projects must have been completed between 1 January 2023 and 14th August 2026 (except for temporary projects).



Temporary Projects Eligibility: Pop-ups and temporary exhibitions must have been completed since 1 January 2024.



Entry Limits: You may submit any project into more than one category if it fits the brief for those categories. The judges may move projects to another category if they think it is more appropriate.



Copyright: You are responsible for providing copyright information for all images included in your entries. Any uncredited images will, by default, be credited to the designer or architect. Please ensure all collaborators are credited for their work where appropriate. By submitting material, you acknowledge that World Interiors News and its partners have the right to reproduce any imagery you provide in whole or part without paying copyright fees.

Help: If you have difficulty uploading your submission, please contact us directly at morgan.lovegrove@haymarket.com and we will assist you.

KEEP UP-TO-DATE WITH WORLD INTERIORS NEWS ON SOCIALS

Copyright © 2026 Haymarket Media Group Limited. All Rights Reserved.

关于.text:
ABOUT THE BRAND

World Interiors News (WIN) and World Architecture News (WAN) are premier online platforms dedicated to showcasing excellence in architecture and interior design from around the globe. 

For over 16 years, WIN and WAN have been the go-to destinations for design professionals seeking inspiration, practical insights and the latest trends. By bringing together innovative projects, expert perspectives, and design breakthroughs, these platforms celebrate creative vision and technical expertise that shape the built environment.

WIN and WAN not only highlight exceptional projects but also foster a global community of architects, interior designers and industry influencers. Through their comprehensive editorial coverage, WIN and WAN provide invaluable resources, including news, in-depth features, and analysis that help professionals stay informed and inspired. 

Trusted by designers and architects worldwide, WIN and WAN continue to drive design discourse, setting the standard for what’s new, relevant and impactful in the world of architecture and interiors.




VISIT THE WORLD INTERIORS NEWS WEBSITE
KEEP UP-TO-DATE WITH WORLD INTERIORS NEWS ON SOCIALS

Copyright © 2026 Haymarket Media Group Limited. All Rights Reserved.

FAQ.text:
Frequently asked questions

We know preparing your entry takes time and thought, so we’ve put together a few frequently asked questions to help guide you through the process and craft a winning submission. 

Please download the entry kit for all information you need. 

If you have further questions please email morgan.lovegrove@haymarket.com.

ABOUT THE AWARDS
Who can enter the Awards?

The awards are open to interior designers, architects, developers, retailers, hoteliers, restaurateurs, manufacturers, and suppliers.

Why should I enter?

There are a number of real benefits to entering these Awards. You can find out more about why you should enter here.

How do I find out if I've won?

The winners will be revealed in November 2026.

Note: Results will be listed on the WIN website, socials and in the WIN/WAN editorial bulletin.

Instagram can be found here.

LinkedIn can be found here.

Editorial can be found here.




How can I get in touch?

For assistance, you can contact Morgan Lovegrove, at morgan.lovegrove@haymarket.com.

Can I see the past winners?

Of course!

Please see our 2025 winners here, our 2024 winners here and our 2023 winners here.

ENTERING THE AWARDS
How do I enter the awards?

You must complete the online entry form on the official website. The submission requires a written entry and supporting materials like images and floor plans depending on the category (ensure copyright info is provided). Please download the entry kit for more information.

Is there a fee to enter?

Yes, the fees are based on the submission date:




First Entry Deadline (7 May 2026): £410 + VAT.

Second Entry Deadline (28 May 2026): £520 + VAT.

Final Entry Deadline (10 August 2026): £560 + VAT.

Can I submit multiple entries?

Yes, you can submit as many entries as you like, provided they meet the eligibility criteria.

What is the deadline for entries?

First Entry Deadline: Thursday 7 May 2026
Second Entry Deadline: Thursday 28 May 2026
Final Entry Deadline: Thursday 6 August 2026

Can I amend my entry or move the category once it's been submitted?

Yes you can log back into the portal here to make minor amends to your entry after submitting. If you submitted at a discounted entry deadline and need to make amends or move categories after this deadline has passed, the higher rate will apply. Here are our T&C's.

When will I receive confirmation of my entry?

You should receive a confirmation email once you complete your registration. This will come from morgan.lovegrove@haymarket.com. Please check your junk mail if you haven’t received it. Please email us if you would like your confirmation email to be resent.

What is your refund policy?

If you wish to withdraw your entry, you must email morgan.lovegrove@haymarket.com. Where you withdraw your entry more than 14 days after booking, or on or after the closing date for entry (whichever is earlier) there will be no refund. Here are our T&C's.

Do you offer feedback to all entrants? Even if we aren't shortlisted?

Yes, feedback is available upon request after the awards are announced.

Do you allow the use of AI?

We acknowledge that the use of AI has become increasingly common, transforming how people work and the outputs they deliver. In light of this, we expect entrants to indicate clearly where AI has been used - whether in the development of the work itself or in shaping the written entry or any associated assets submitted as part of that entry.

Will my entry be published? I have confidential information within it

We would not publish your entry without prior permission. If you were shortlisted, we will publish your Executive Summary so we do ask no confidential information is included within this. If you win, we would do a write up on your entry, but this shouldn't include any confidential information. You can additionally mark parts of your entry as confidential/not for publication to ensure this, provided such restrictions are not used unreasonably.

What is your Entry Code of Conduct?

Haymarket Events, part of Haymarket Media Group, is committed to ensuring that all entrants and entries are judged fairly, equitably, and by their peers. With this in mind, we would like to remind all entrants that we expect them to enter their work in good faith, with honesty and integrity.

We expect all entries to represent bona fide work that a company or agency is proud to have produced.

We acknowledge that the use of AI has become increasingly common, transforming how people work and the outputs they deliver. In light of this, we expect entrants to indicate clearly where AI has been used — whether in the development of the work itself or in shaping the written entry or any associated assets submitted as part of that entry.

If it becomes clear that an entry, or if the work entered, has been fabricated in any way, whether intentionally or unintentionally, Haymarket Media Group reserves the right to disqualify the entry.

If results have already been published, we retain the right to rescind the award and will publish an explanation on our relevant brand sites. Please note: any such decision will be made in consultation with the Chair of Judges and/or relevant judging panel.

Haymarket Media Group stands by its values of creativity, expertise, integrity, innovation, and respect. We expect everyone who enters, judges, or attends our events to uphold these same principles.

JUDGING PROCESS
Who are the judges?

The panel consists of leading experts and professionals from the interior design and architecture industry. The Chair of Judges for this 2026 cycle is Ruud Belmans and a full list of judges can be found here.

How can I become a judge?

You can nominate yourself or a colleague here for review.

How are the entries judged?

Entries are judged based on a set of criteria outlined for each award category - you can find out more in the Entry Kit here. The judging process is rigorous and involves multiple rounds of review to ensure fairness and accuracy. The judges' decisions are final.

Is the judging process confidential?

Yes, the judging process is strictly confidential. Judges are bound by a non-disclosure agreement, and all information submitted in your entry is kept confidential.

If I'm a judge can my company or I still enter the Awards?

Of course! We would just ensure you weren't judging your own entry or categories you entered to maintain the integrity of the judging process.

ACCESSIBILITY
What is your overall commitment to accessibility?

We ask all our attendees if they have any additional requirements as part of the booking process and make every effort to accommodate them. We're committed to making this event accessible to everyone. If you have any further questions about the accessibility of the event or if there's anything we may be able to arrange please contact eventaccessibility@haymarket.com who can support you in with your access needs and getting any necessary adjustments made.

How can I communicate my specific access needs?

Please contact eventaccessibility@haymarket.com who can support you in with your access needs and getting any necessary adjustments made.

SUSTAINABILITY
What is your overall commitment to sustainability?

Haymarket Media Group is committed to playing our part in creating a more sustainable future and are working hard to minimise the carbon footprint of our events. We want to create more sustainable events and help our attendees do the same. You can find out more here.

KEEP UP-TO-DATE WITH WORLD INTERIORS NEWS ON SOCIALS

Carrville Community Centre, Library, and District Park

Perkins&Will

Net-zero civic hub uniting city and park. “The overall design is impressive, with all forms and settings subordinating to the user's function, maximizing people-oriented and community-building.”, Dang Ming, WIN Awards Judge Carrville Community Centre, Library, and District Park sets out to knit a growing neighbourhood to its landscape ...

Tamrin Space

Cando Archineering Group

A sensitive restoration transforming an 80-year-old Tehran building into an accessible, multi-use home for music and theatre, with daylight, ventilation and civic amenities catalysing community interaction. Conceived with and for music and theatre practitioners, Tamrin Space reimagines a dilapidated 80-year-old house in Tehran’s cultur...

BMO Centre Expansion

Populous + Stantec + S2 Architecture

Reimagining Western Electric’s 1931 headquarters, Watertown Exploratory Labs ...

BRONZE

Historic Teahouse Reimagined as Boutique Hotel.Adaptive reuse of a 1913 teahouse near Macao's UNESCO zone, blending careful conservation with contemporary hospitality; evocative common areas and storytelling details reconnect the building to harbour heritage. MUSEUMS & GALLERIES

In Your Wildest Dreams, Ensor Beyond Impressionism

Tinker imagineers

In Your Wildest Dreams, Ensor Beyond Impressionism Immersive, interactive scenography reimagines the exhibition “Impressive attempt at instilling new and interactive materials to showcasing what is typically a static artwork exhibition.”, Azlan Nazam, WIN Awards JudgeCommissioned as the culmination of Belgium’s Year of Ensor, the Ro...

Read More

FINALIST
Harbourside Display Gallery

Mirvac Design

TRANSPORT
GOLD
Portland International Airport, Main Terminal Expansion

WIN Awards 2026 - Judges Nomination Form

We invite you to nominate yourself or a colleague using the form below. The final judging panel will be comprised of an inclusive range of individuals to fully represent the industry. Nominations to get involved are welcome from across the sectors.

Please complete the following form to receive information about the WIN Awards 2026 judging.

1. Are you submitting this nomination for yourself or are you nominating a colleague? *
This question is required.
Myself
Colleague
2. Have you been a judge for these awards in the past, if yes, what year? *
This question is required.
No
Yes- 2024
Yes- 2023
Yes- 2022
Yes- 2021
Yes- Other 
Please enter an 'other' value for this selection.
3. Judges Personal Details *
This question is required.
First Name
Last Name
Job Title
Company Name
Work Email Address
This question requires a valid email address.
Work Phone Number
Secondary email address, this can be a EA/PA or a personal email address! *
This question is required.
4. Are you/your company interested in entering the awards?
Yes
No
5. 50 Word Personal Biography *
This question is required.

Privacy Notice

Haymarket Media Group, organisers of WIN Awards 2026 takes your privacy seriously. We'll use your personal information to provide the products and services you have requested.

We will use your information to ensure you receive messages that are relevant to you. You can stop receiving these messages from us at any time in the future via the unsubscribe link on any email received, or by contacting us directly. Please see our Full Privacy Notice for more information about how we use your information.
